projectDescription: A drop-in SwiftUI component that adds sticky headers with progressive blur — like Apple Music, Photos, and App Store — where content scrolls underneath with increasing blur, never clipped.
keyFeatures: Drop-in SwiftUI component with a single-line integration
Progressive blur using Apple's own private API (via VariableBlur)
Content never clipped — always visible underneath the header
Automatic header height measurement via GeometryReader and PreferenceKey
Tint overlay that adapts to light/dark mode automatically
Fully customizable: blur radius, fade extension, and tint opacity
App Store approved — uses the same blur API as Apple's own apps
publicApiSurface: init(maxBlurRadius: CGFloat = 5, fadeExtension: CGFloat = 64, tintOpacityTop: Double = 0.7, tintOpacityMiddle: Double = 0.5, header: () -> Header, content: () -> Content)
maxBlurRadius: CGFloat — Maximum blur intensity at the top edge (default: 5)
fadeExtension: CGFloat — How far blur extends below the header in points (default: 64)
tintOpacityTop: Double — Tint darkness at the very top behind Dynamic Island (default: 0.7)
tintOpacityMiddle: Double — Tint darkness at the header's vertical center (default: 0.5)
